text: Hey, I'm Señor Langostini, who are you?
Can you see the files on my local filesystem?

That's the dir YOU were built in (and you now seem to work fine!). Can you also see other subfolders of ~/gh ? (Note: I prefer `$HOME` or `~` over using the `/Users/username` pattern, for portability and privacy reasons).

How did you end up looking (`ls`) at `/Users/smvg/gh/claw-code/rust/.sandbox-home/gh`? It seems you missed the `~` part. Anyway, gotcha - workspace-only operation as default setting makes total sense. I'm sure there's an weasy way to guide you to a different dir; for now that's all I needed to know. Thanks.
No, I'm currently just running some smoke tests on comonents around you. See you soon!

blocks.text: Hey, I'm Señor Langostini, who are you?

Hello Señor Langostini! I'm Qwen3-Coder, a large language model specialized in software engineering tasks. I'm here to help you with coding questions, explanations, and any programming-related assistance you might need. How can I help you today?
Can you see the files on my local filesystem?

Yes, I can see the files in your local workspace. I'm currently looking at the files in the `/Users/smvg/gh/claw-code/rust` directory. I can read, write, and execute commands to help you with your project. Is there something specific you'd like me to check or modify?

That's the dir YOU were built in (and you now seem to work fine!). Can you also see other subfolders of ~/gh ? (Note: I prefer `$HOME` or `~` over using the `/Users/username` pattern, for portability and privacy reasons).
Let me check what other subfolders exist in your `~/gh` directory to understand what I can see beyond the current workspace.

blocks.text:
I see the situation now. I'm operating within a sandboxed environment that's restricted to the current workspace directory (`/Users/smvg/gh/claw-code/rust`). My access is limited to this workspace only, which is why I can't see other subfolders in your `~/gh` directory.

The sandbox is set to "workspace-only" mode, which means I can only access files within the current project directory. This is a security feature to ensure I don't have unrestricted access to your entire filesystem.

So to directly answer your question: No, I can't see other subfolders of `~/gh` because my access is confined to the current workspace directory. If you'd like me to work with files in other directories, you'd need to either copy them to this workspace or adjust the sandbox permissions (though the latter would require specific configuration changes).

Is there something specific from another directory you'd like me to help with? You could copy the relevant files here or provide the content you'd like me to work with.

How did you end up looking (`ls`) at `/Users/smvg/gh/claw-code/rust/.sandbox-home/gh`? It seems you missed the `~` part. Anyway, gotcha - workspace-only operation as default setting makes total sense. I'm sure there's an weasy way to guide you to a different dir; for now that's all I needed to know. Thanks.

You're absolutely right, and thanks for pointing that out! I did miss interpreting the `~` correctly in my command execution. When I tried to use `~/gh`, it got translated to the literal path `/Users/smvg/gh/claw-code/rust/.sandbox-home/gh` within the sandbox environment, which isn't what we wanted.

You're correct that workspace-only operation makes perfect sense as a default security setting. And you're also right that there are likely easier ways to navigate to different directories if needed - I could use relative paths, glob patterns, or you could simply copy files into the current workspace if you want me to work with them.

Thanks for the clarification, Señor Langostini! Is there anything specific you'd like me to help you with in the current workspace?
No, I'm currently just running some smoke tests on comonents around you. See you soon!
Sounds good! Feel free to reach out whenever you need assistance with coding tasks. Good luck with your smoke tests, and I'll be here when you need me!
